title: Bevan French

{{short description|Australian professional rugby league footballer}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2016}} {{Use Australian English|date=August 2016}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Bevan French |fullname = Bevan Lionel French |image = Bevan French Wigan.jpg |imagesize = |birth_date = {{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|1|4}} |birth_place = [[Inverell, New South Wales|Inverell]], New South Wales, Australia |height = {{convert|5|ft|9|in|m|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|86|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} |position = {{rlp|SO|FB|WG}} |club1 = [[Parramatta Eels]] |year1start = 2016 |year1end = 18 |appearances1 = 47 |tries1 = 35 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 140 |club2 = [[Wigan Warriors]] |year2start = 2019 |year2end = present |appearances2 = 140 |tries2 = 118 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 1 |points2 = 541 |teamA = [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] |yearAstart = 2017 |yearAend = 19 |appearancesA = 2 |triesA = 1 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 4 |retired = |source = {{Rugby league records|title=Bevan French|id=4121|access-date=6 April 2023}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/bevan-french/summary.html|title=Bevan French - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=Rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=2017-03-30}} |updated = 23 April 2025 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Nathan Blacklock]] (uncle)
[[Preston Campbell]] (cousin) }} }} '''Bevan French''' (born 4 January 1996) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays primarily as a {{rlp|so}}, but also as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]]. French began his career playing for amateur club [[Tingha, New South Wales|Tingha]] Tigers, playing first team rugby at age 15. In 2013, he signed for [[Parramatta Eels]] in the [[NRL]] making his first team debut the year after. In 2019, he left Parramatta for [[Wentworthville Magpies]] in the [[NSW Cup]] where he played for six months before joining Wigan. Internationally, he has represented the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]]. With Wigan, French has won two Super League Grand Finals in [[2023 Super League Grand Final|2023]], and [[2024 Super League Grand Final|2024]], two Challenge Cup finals in [[2022 Challenge Cup final|2022]] and [[2024 Challenge Cup final|2024]], in addition to the World Club Challenge in [[2024 World Club Challenge|2024]]. The 2024 campaign saw him become the first player in British rugby league history to win Man of the Match in all three finals. ==Background== French was born in [[Inverell]], New South Wales, Australia, and is of [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]] descent. He is the nephew of former [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian]] international and [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] winger [[Nathan Blacklock]].{{citation needed|date=July 2023}} He played his junior rugby league for the [[Tingha Tigers]]. French was educated and 2013 graduate at Inverell High School.{{citation needed|date=May 2024}} ==Playing career== ===Early career=== At the age of 15, French began playing for Tingha's first-grade team at fullback, playing against men twice his age.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/huge-wrap-on-parramatta-eels-17yearold-bevan-french-who-six-nrl-clubs-were-chasing/story-fni3fbgz-1226792194267|title=Huge wrap on Parramatta Eels' 17-year-old Bevan French, who six NRL clubs were chasing|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=2017-03-30}} Early in 2013, French was pursued by six NRL clubs; [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]], [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]], [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]], the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]], [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] and [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]]. He eventually signed for the Parramatta Eels, stating that he chose the club because he "just had a feeling". ===Parramatta Eels=== ====2015==== [[File:Bevan French.jpg|thumb|right|French playing for the Parramatta Eels in 2015]] In 2015 and 2016, he played for the Eels' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2016/05/17/r11_nyc_team_list.html|title=Team List: Eels NYC v Storm - Eels|publisher=Parraeels.com.au|date=17 May 2016|accessdate=2016-05-31}} In 2015, he began the season at five-eighth, before moving back to fullback for the remainder of the year, finishing the season with 16 tries in 22 games.{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/f/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304035513/http://nycdatabase.org/players/f/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=4 March 2016|title=F|publisher=Nyc Database|date=27 December 2012|accessdate=2016-05-31}} On 11 July 2015, he re-signed with the Parramatta club on a three-year contract to the end of the 2018 season.{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/eels-re-sign-seven-players-18135/#DHpxvcQuL9lg7X2X.97|title=Eels re-sign seven players|date=11 July 2015|website=Zerotackle.com|accessdate=2017-03-30|archive-date=25 June 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160625043608/http://www.zerotackle.com/eels-re-sign-seven-players-18135/#DHpxvcQuL9lg7X2X.97|url-status=dead}} ====2016==== In February, French participated in Eels' [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] tournament win, scoring 8 tries in the tournament, the record for most tries in a Nines tournament.{{cite web|author=Michael Carayannis|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/auckland-nines-parramatta-eels-beat-new-zealand-warriors-to-claim-auckland-nines-20160207-gmnqcd.html|title=Auckland Nines: Parramatta Eels beat New Zealand Warriors to claim title|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=2016-02-07|accessdate=2016-05-31}}{{cite web|author=Chris Barrett|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-eels-young-gun-bevan-french-in-line-for-nrl-debut-against-newcastle-20160529-gp6oo8.html|title=Parramatta Eels young gun Bevan French in line for NRL debut against Newcastle|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=29 May 2016|accessdate=2016-05-31}} He was also named in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines#Team of the tournament|team of the tournament]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/auckland-nines-team-of-the-tournament/tabid/10874/newsid/92718/default.aspx|title=Auckland Nines Team of the Tournament|publisher=NRL.com|date=7 February 2016|accessdate=2016-05-31}} In Round 12 of the [[2016 NRL season]], French made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut against the [[Newcastle Knights]], scoring a try in the 20-18 win at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/foran-injured-as-eels-hold-off-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/96491/default.aspx |title=Foran injured as Eels hold off Knights |website=NRL.com |date= 30 May 2016|accessdate=2017-03-30}}{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/parramatta-eels-young-gun-bevan-french-in-line-for-nrl-debut-against-newcastle-20160529-gp6oo8.html|title=Parramatta Eels young gun Bevan French in line for NRL debut against Newcastle|website=Smh.com.au|date=29 May 2016 |accessdate=2017-03-30}} In Round 15, against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], French scored his first double in Parramatta's 30-12 win at [[ANZ Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2016-parramatta-eels-keep-finals-hope-alive-with-3012-win-over-south-sydney-rabbitohs/news-story/1a6dd876f1521ea935ead0032cd67ced |title=Parramatta Eels Keep Finals Hope Alive With 30-12 Win Over South Sydney |website=Dailytelegraph.com.au |date= |accessdate=2017-03-30}} In Round 17, against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], French scored his first [[hat-trick]] of tries in Parramatta's 34-24 loss at [[Shark Park]].{{cite web|author= |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/parramatta-eels/parramatta-eels-bevan-french-matched-only-by-israel-folau-as-home-visit-sparks-tryscoring-spree-20160703-gpxb6l.html |title=Parramatta Eels' Bevan French matched only by Israel Folau as home visit sparks try-scoring spree |website=Smh.com.au |date=2016-07-03 |accessdate=2017-03-30}} In the last two matches of the regular season, French switched with [[Michael Gordon (rugby league)|Michael Gordon]] to play from the wing to fullback, scoring two hat-tricks in two weeks in a row in Parramatta's 30-18 win against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and 40-18 win against the [[New Zealand Warriors]].{{cite web|author= |url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/bevan-french-scores-a-hat-trick-as-parramatta-eels-thump-warriors/news-story/b81ca65bae6ad1184b6cd6c11221071b |title=French bags three as Eels thump Warriors |website=News.com.au |date=2016-09-04 |accessdate=2017-03-30}} French quickly went on to be a try-scoring sensation in his debut season the NRL, being the club's highest try-scorer with 19 tries in 13 matches in the [[2016 NRL season]]. ====2017==== On 10 February 2017, French represented the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] against the [[World All Stars]] in the [[2017 All Stars match]], playing off the interchange bench in the 34-8 win at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-thump-world-all-stars-348-in-newcastle/news-story/a016f102b1c7ddc5bbdeeedb83a149c5 |title=Indigenous All-Stars Thump World All-Stars 34-8 |website=Dailytelegraph.com.au |date= |accessdate=2017-03-30}} French played 16 games for Parramatta in the 2017 season scoring 9 tries but missed most of the season with a troubling [[hamstring]] injury. French injured himself just before the start of the finals series, Parramatta's first in eight seasons and missed both finals games against Melbourne and North Queensland.https://m.nrl.com/eels-v-cowboys-schick-preview/tabid/10874/newsid/112083/default.aspx{{Dead link|date=July 2020 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Ny0xNjk2LTQtLS0tLS0yMjc3NC0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List - RLP|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/nrl-2017-parramatta-eels-defeat-gold-coast-titans-but-lose-bevan-french-to-hamstring-injury-20170817-gxytuq.html|title = NRL 2017: Parramatta Eels defeat Gold Coast Titans but lose Bevan French to hamstring injury|date = 17 August 2017}} ====2018==== French started the 2018 season at fullback ahead of Clint Gutherson who was still out with an injury and the returning Jarryd Hayne. In round 3 of the season, French injured his shoulder in the 14-4 loss to Cronulla and missed the following two games. He was then recalled to the side to face Canberra but was slammed by fans and the media after the match due to the fact that he only made one run for the whole game making 15 metres. French was dropped by coach Brad Arthur for the following week. French spoke of his demotion saying "We spoke about how my shoulder was going to be in pain going into the game and getting a needle before the game that was supposedly meant to numb it for six hours or something, But 15 minutes in I felt it. Because of that I didn't really get into the game and it was playing in the back of my mind, Injury did play a part in my poor performance but it was form as well. Brad pulled me into his office at training and sort of explained why I was dropped and I couldn't blame him, it was the right thing to do."{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/04/26/parramattas-bevan-french-says-an-ineffective-pain-killing-injection-contributed-to-his-axing/|title=French: Ineffective pain-killer contributed to axing|date=26 April 2018}} On 2 June 2018, French was named at fullback for Parramatta's round 13 clash against Newcastle but was taken from the field only two minutes into the match due to a nasty head collision involving Newcastle player [[Kalyn Ponga]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-parramatta-eels-v-newcastle-knights-live-score-updates-news-blog/news-story/96dc846388bfa8e69be2f00f8414bdde|title = The Eels' horror season has hit a new low, losing Bevan French and Mitchell Moses in the first half of their loss to the Knights|date = 2 June 2018}} On 13 July 2018, Parramatta who were in last place on the ladder played against Newcastle and with 34 seconds remaining French crossed over to score the match winning try only for the decision to be overturned due to video replays showing French had put a foot into touch while grounding the ball. Newcastle held on to win the game 18-16.{{Cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2018-07-13/nrl-sharks-beat-panthers-knights-edge-out-eels/9985126|title = Moylan helps Sharks topple Panthers, Knights pip Eels|newspaper = ABC News|date = 13 July 2018}} On 7 August, French was demoted to reserve grade by coach [[Brad Arthur]] after a number of mixed performances. It came in the wake of French speaking to the media a week earlier declaring he was open to the idea of leaving Parramatta if he was not given a chance to play fullback.{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/french-becomes-the-latest-eels-big-name-to-feel-the-axe-20180807-p4zw3i.html|title = French becomes the latest Eels big name to feel the axe|date = 7 August 2018}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/bevan-french-open-to-leaving-eels-to-pursue-fullback-position-as-rival-club-circles/news-story/3cccb9038e0b7dbc778808b1e0eeccda|title = Bevan French open to leaving Eels to pursue fullback position as rival club circles|date = August 2018}} ===Wentworthville Magpies=== ====2019==== [[File:Bevan French Wentworthville.jpg|thumb|right|French playing for the Wentworthville Magpies in 2019]] French was left out of the Parramatta's 2019 NRL squad, with head coach [[Brad Arthur]] selecting [[Blake Ferguson (rugby league)|Blake Ferguson]] and [[Maika Sivo]] for wing and [[Clint Gutherson]] for fullback. This saw French play for [[Wentworthville Magpies]], one of Parramatta's feeder clubs, during the first half on the 2019 season. On 22 April 2019, French scored the first ever try at the new [[Western Sydney Stadium]] for the [[Wentworthville Magpies]] against [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] in a 20-14 victory.{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2019/04/23/magpies-record-first-ever-win-at-bankwest-stadium/|title=Magpies record first ever win at Bankwest Stadium|website=Parramatta Eels|date=22 April 2019}} [[File:Bevan French Wentworthville Magpies.jpg|thumb|right|French playing for Wentworthville in 2019]] French made 15 appearances and scored 13 tries for Wentworthville in the [[NSW Cup]] during his time at the club.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49139147|title=Wigan Warriors sign versatile Parramatta Eels back|work=BBC Sport }}{{cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/players/nsw-cup/wentworthville-magpies/bevan-french/|title=Player profile|website=NSWRL|access-date=29 July 2019|archive-date=29 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190729083846/https://www.nswrl.com.au/players/nsw-cup/wentworthville-magpies/bevan-french/|url-status=dead}} ===Wigan Warriors=== ====2019==== On 27 July, French signed a two-year contract with [[Super League]] side [[Wigan Warriors]]. French made his debut appearance for Wigan against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] coming off the bench in a 36-18 victory.{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/wigan-warriors-fans-get-first-glimpse-of-electrifying-bevan-french-1-9927570/|title=Wigan Warriors fans get first glimpse of "electrifying" Bevan French|website=Wigan Today}} ====2020==== French played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] for Wigan which the club lost 8-4 against St Helens. With the scores at 4-4 and less than ten seconds on the clock, St Helens attempted a long range field goal which hit the right hand upright. The ball bounced past French and came into the hands of [[Jack Welsby]] who scored the winning try of the game.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }} ====2021==== After missing the opening three rounds of the [[2021 Super League season]], French returned for Wigan's round 4 match and scored two tries in a 22-12 victory over Castleford.{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-player-ratings-french-20447371/|title=Wigan Warriors player ratings|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=22 April 2021|archive-date=22 April 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422190647/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-player-ratings-french-20447371|url-status=dead}} French injured his hamstring in the round 7 match against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]. French was initially ruled out for twelve weeks with a torn hamstring.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12321566/zak-hardaker-wigan-warriors-full-back-given-two-match-ban-after-red-card-for-headbutt/|title=Wigan Warriors full-back given two-match ban after red card for headbutt|website=www.skysports.com}} [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] later put out a statement, on 28 June, clarifying that French’s injury was worse than first expected. He was ruled out for the [[2021 Super League season]] and would return to Australia, to be with family during his rehabilitation. At the same time, French triggered a clause in his contract meaning that he would remain at the club for the 2022 season.{{Cite web|date=2021-06-28|title=Update: Bevan French - Wigan Warriors|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2021/06/28/update-bevan-french/|access-date=2021-06-28|website=Wigan Warriors Blog}} ====2022==== On 28 May, French played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] win over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }} In round 15 of the 2022 Super League season, French scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 30-12 victory over [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bevan-french-inspires-wigan-win-salford/|title=Salford 12-30 Wigan: Bevan French hat-trick inspires Warriors to big win|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=10 June 2022 }} In round 18, French scored two tries for Wigan in a 20-18 loss against St Helens at [[Magic Weekend]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live-blog/12196/12648675/super-league-magic-weekend-day-1-at-newcastles-st-james-park-live/|title=Saints, Toulouse and Leeds all win on Magic Weekend|website=www.skysports.com}} The following week, French scored a Super League record seven tries in Wigan's 60-0 victory over [[Hull F.C.]] to overtake the six tries that [[Lesley Vainikolo]] scored against coincidentally Hull F.C. in 2005.{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live-wigan-warriors-v-hull-7340022/|title=LIVE Wigan Warriors v Hull FC live updates and match action|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=15 July 2022|archive-date=17 July 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220717231055/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live-wigan-warriors-v-hull-7340022|url-status=dead}} In round 21, French scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 46-4 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12660812/super-league-bevan-french-and-liam-marshall-star-for-wigan-warriors-in-46-4-win-over-hull-kr/|title=Super League: Bevan French and Liam Marshall star for Wigan Warriors in 46-4 win over Hull KR|website=www.skysports.com}} In round 24, French scored another [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 52-6 victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-52-6-toulouse-bevan-french-scores-hat-trick-as-warriors-strengthen-second-spot/|title=Wigan 52-6 Toulouse: Bevan French scores hat-trick as Warriors strengthen second spot|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=19 August 2022 }} On 4 September, French made the Super League dream team.{{cite web | url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12689882/super-league-dream-team-2022-salford-red-devils-brodie-croft-among-six-newcomers-this-year | title=Super League Dream Team 2022: Salford Red Devils' Brodie Croft among six newcomers this year }} French finished the regular season with 33 tries in 23 appearances for Wigan. On 20 September 2022, Wigan confirmed that French had signed a new two-year contract with the club, keeping him at the club until 2024. ====2023==== In round 2 of the 2023 Super League season, French scored two tries for Wigan in a 60-0 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12818901/wigan-warriors-60-0-wakefield-trinity-liam-marshall-scores-four-tries-as-wigan-run-riot-against-wakefield/|title=Wigan Warriors 60-0 Wakefield Trinity: Liam Marshall scores four tries as Wigan run riot against Wakefield|website=www.skysports.com}} In round 5, French scored two tries as Wigan won a narrow game against Huddersfield 14-12.{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/bevan-french-scores-brace-as-wigan-warriors-overcome-huddersfield-giants-in-close-contest-4069847/|title=Warriors overcome Huddersfield Giants in close contest|website=www.wigantoday.net}} In Round 6, Wigan again had a French double to thank as they left it late to beat Salford at home. French scored for Wigan in games against Leigh and Wakefield over the Easter period. In the Challenge Cup Last 16 away to Leeds the following week, French scored twice and crucially denied [[Harry Newman (rugby league)|Harry Newman]] a certain try as Wigan came back to win 18-14. French scored in defeats to both the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Magic Weekend]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. In round 20, French scored two tries in Wigan's 44-18 victory over Leigh.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/jul/29/wigan-leigh-super-league-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Abbas Miski's hat-trick leads mauling as Wigan leapfrog Leigh in Super League|newspaper=The Observer|date=29 July 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} In October French was named as the winner of the 2023 [[Man of Steel Awards|Steve Prescott MBE Man of Steel award]].{{cite web |title=Bevan French: Wigan Warriors stand-off wins 2023 Steve Prescott MBE Man of Steel award |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67070647 |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=11 October 2023 |date=10 October 2023}} On 14 October, French played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} ====2024==== [[File:Bevan French Wigan Warriors.jpg|thumb|right|French pre-match for Wigan in 2024]] On 24 February, French played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]], and was awarded Man of the Match.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|work=BBC Sport |date=24 February 2024 }} French scored Wigan's only try in their 12–4 [[Good Friday Derby]] defeat against [[St. Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{cite web | url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2024/03/29/wigan-lose-out-in-the-derby/ | title=Wigan lose out in the Derby | date=29 March 2024 }} On 3 April, French signed a new four-year deal with the club.{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13107127/bevan-french-wigan-warriors-secure-reigning-super-league-man-of-steel-for-four-more-years|title=Bevan French: Wigan Warriors secure reigning Super League Man of Steel for four more years|website=Sky Sports}} On 8 June, French started for Wigan in [[2024 Challenge Cup final]]. He scored one try in the game (plus another ruled out by VR) in the 18–8 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], and was awarded the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] as man of the match.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=8 June 2024 }} French finish the season with Wigan retaining both the [[League Leaders' Shield]] and Super League title, winning the [[Rob Burrow Award]] in the [[2024 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]], having scored the only try of the match.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cvgdzjx0l1ko|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors 9-2 Hull KR|date=12 October 2024|website=BBC Sport}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/oct/12/wigan-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report|title=Wigan secure back-to-back titles after Bevan French’s stunner sinks Hull KR|first=Aaron|last=Bower|date=12 October 2024|via=The Guardian}} [[Sky Sports]] praised French at the end of the season for consistent ability to change the course of a game with individual skill.{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/13231913/the-wigan-warriors-dynasty-what-makes-the-super-league-champions-tick|title=The Wigan Warriors dynasty: What makes the Super League champions tick?|website=Sky Sports}} The 2024 season saw French become the first player in British rugby league history to win both the Lance Todd Trophy and Harry Sunderland / Rob Burrow Award in the same season,{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/the-astonishing-feat-achieved-by-wigan-warriors-superstar-bevan-french|title=The astonishing feat achieved by Wigan Warriors superstar Bevan French|date=13 October 2024|website=Love Rugby League}} having done so also winning the Bill Ashurst medal in the 2024 World Club Challenge.{{Cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2024/10/13/french-on-grand-final/|title=French on Grand Final|first=Charlotte|last=Fletcher|date=13 October 2024|website=Wigan Warriors}} ====2025==== French was a standout player in Wigan's [[Rugby League Las Vegas#2025 event|Vegas]] 48–24 win over Warrington, scoring one try and winning player of the match.{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2025/03/02/french-flair-on-show-as-wigan-keep-knack-for-big-game-performances-intact/|title=French flair on show as Wigan nail another big occasion|date=2 March 2025|website=National Rugby League}} On 9 October, French played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ====2026==== In round four of the 2026 season, French tore his [[hamstring]] ruling him out for four months.https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c8752n94d5jo On 30 May, French played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship/title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}} ==Personal life== During his absence due to injury being midway through 2021 season, French returned home to Australia for his rehabilitation due to his mother's [[Motor Neurone Disease]] worsening. She died during this time. French was granted compassionate leave by Wigan, returning to the UK two months into the 2022 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/bevan-french-admits-challenge-cup-win-was-pretty-emotional-after-losing-his-mum-to-mnd-4658497|title=Bevan French admits Challenge Cup win was 'pretty emotional' - after losing his mum to MND|date=8 June 2024|website=Wigan Today}} ==Honours== ===Wigan Warriors=== *'''[[Super League Grand Final|Super League]]''' **'''Winners (2):''' [[2023
